mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent housing market signals indicate structural demand fragmentation; declining mortgage applications combined with elevated housing starts suggest inventory buildup that will compress corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, sustained industrial production growth reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain flat or negative during periods of sustained INDPRO expansion, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly proxies aggregate economic demand, and higher GDP expansion rates mechanically increase corporate cash flow expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of GDPC1 growth against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows, indicating no persistent predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate breaches a 4.5% threshold, borrowing costs exceed the sustainable profitability margin for highly leveraged equities, triggering a valuation compression that generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances following the rate threshold breach, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.1 over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate acceleration signal a synchronized labor market cooling that precedes earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the joint signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: During a stagflationary regime, persistent month-over-month CPI acceleration erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral during sustained CPI acceleration episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 48% over a 5-year rolling window.
